violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Bagged meatballs was observed held at (90) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC reheated food product to 180 degrees F. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Various food products was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the prep units and 2 dr refrigerator unit refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The presence of rodents and fruit flies is not controlled as evidenced by ( rodent droppings and live activity of fruit flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Inspector observed dead rodent on glue trap.PIC removed from premise. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
Inspector observed towel being used to cover produce.PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Wood is used in the construction of food storage shelves in the walk in cooler area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Prep unit located in the kitchen area was not in good repair. Inspector observed ambient temperatures at 46 degrees F. Establishment not allowed to store TCS foods in unit until repaired/or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces of the have an accumulation debris: 1.shelves over 3 bay sink has soil residue and other debris. 2.shelves located downstairs near walk in cooler area observed with rodent droppings. 3.storage bin observed with debris and soil residue. 4.prep units have food residue and other debris, 5.shelves in the walk in cooler have food and soil residue. fans guards in walk in cooler observed with dust. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and/or rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
